entry.title: Re: Ignore Hard coding in S4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi sergei_gook

Looks like we missed the comments until recently.

To be honest I am not very familiar with the TVARVC table or those transactions.

But I can say we don't have the table exposed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

If I understood the use case, you would like to, for example, develop a BAdI in Custom Logic to look up a value for a storage location, however if that SL changes in the future, you don't want to change the code, you want to just update a table.

In this type of case, looking up a value mapping for example, it sounds like the perfect use case for a Custom Business Object

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b45696ca0d9143cba040797e9c71aa44.html?locale=en-US

There are lots of blogs and posts in the extensibility community about CBOs as well.

I hope this helps

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Commodity Code
entry.content:
Hi @Anastasia80 ,

You can assign a different numbering scheme to each country in "Assign Numbering Scheme for Commodity Codes to Country/Region" activity. Then, in the Fiori app "Classify products - commodity codes", you can assign different commodity codes to the same material in each numbering scheme.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: SAP IAS and Successfactors - How SAML2 Attributes are...
entry.content: Thanks a lot, I missed the information that NameID is always taken from EntraID 🙂

entry.title: Re: Commodity Code
entry.content: Really???!!!!

entry.title: Re: Web Assistant Product Version update
entry.content: by the way, what would be the recommendation for the future production version to set for the home-made content? Rather set a fixed version (using the mass update feature in Tools - Context Administration - Edit Web Context) or rather clear the version so that the content is then kind of "universal"?

entry.title: Re: Commodity Code
entry.content: Stanislaw thanks a lot for the information!!! I don't understand why the Expert talsk me that it's necessary to do a custom for this!

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner – HCM-Integration - Additional Customer Company Code/Sales Area
entry.content:
With SAP S/4HANA, many organizations are implementing HCM-Business Partner Integration with automatic supplier and customer creation processes. This allows employees to also be maintained as customers in the system.

To get more detailed information for HCM-Business Partner- Integration, please have a look at this blog posts:
New Employee Business Partner Data Model in SAP S/4HANA 2020 On-Premise
External Workforce and Customer Role in New Employee Business Partner Data Model

To use an employee as a "full" customer, sales-dependent data needs to be entered manually.

Here is a way to extend a customer by additional Company Codes and Sales areas.
This scenario is commonly used in consumer industries where an employee buys products from different organizational units within the company.

For example, an employee employed in Company Code DE01 may want to buy products from both DE01 (e.g. phones) and DE02 (e.g. displays), where DE01 and DE02 are related to the same enterprise.

The standard behavior of Employee-Business Partner-Customer integration only creates Company Code data for the employee's own Company Code, without Sales Area data.

To extend the customer master data, you can use a Business Add-In (BAdI) that is called during the HCM-Business Partner synchronization process.
Based on a custom control table and data from a reference Business Partner, you can extend the current employee customer during synchronization to include additional Company Codes and Sales Areas. This allows the employee to be maintained as a customer across multiple organizational units, beyond just their assigned Company Code.

The BAdI /SHCM/B_EP_BP_ROLE_SYNC can be used to modify customer data including general data, company code information, roles, etc. during the synchronization process. Report /SHCM/R_EMPL_HANDLE_BPROLES is used to handle the role assignments for employee Business Partners.

By leveraging these customization options, you can extend the standard employee-customer integration to support more complex organizational scenarios where employees need to be maintained as customers across multiple Company Codes and Sales Areas. This provides more flexibility in how employee-customers are managed in S/4HANA.

 

Prerequisites

This functionality is available starting from SAP S/4HANA 2022 FPS0 with the active Business Function /SHCM/EE_BP_1, which enables the New Employee Business Partner Model.

 

Activate Customer Synchronization Process

In the customization view PTRV_VENDOR_SYNC (Synchronize HR data to supplier), the option "Customer" or "Customer and Vendor" must be activated.

You can access this view via transaction SM30 or through IMG: Personnel Management -> SAP S/4HANA for Human Resources -> Synchronize Business Partners with Active Business Partner Integration -> Define Default Data for Employees in Role FI Supplier/Customer.

 

Verify Employee Master Data

To check the organizational assignment, use transaction PA20 to view the employee master data and navigate to the Infotype "Organizational assignment."

As illustrated, employee 56 is assigned to Company Code DE01. In conjunction with the customization view PTRV_VENDOR_SYNC, the Business Partner for employee 56 will be extended to include customer and vendor roles with Company Code data for DE01. The primary purpose is to use vendor data for travel expenses and customer data as baseline information for customer processes.

You can verify all related employee objects linked to HCM-Business Partner integration by using transaction /SHCM/EE_BP_RLTN.

Upon investigation, you will see that the employment Business Partner includes only the Financial Roles FLCU00 and FLVN00, alongside role BUP010.

 

Custom Control Table

I opted to use a custom control table to determine which employee Company Codes will require the creation of additional Company Codes and Sales Areas. Here's an example of my custom table:

Perhaps you noticed that the Employee Company Code and Additional Company Code are the same, in this case, DE01. This setup is intentional for usability purposes, providing a clear view of Company Code and Sales Area assignments.

 

Extend Reference Business Partner

The Reference Business Partner (in my case EE00000000) is crucial for defining default values used during data synchronization/creation. Ensure the Reference Business Partner is extended with relevant Company Codes and Sales Areas. In my scenario, I added Company Code DE02 to both customer and vendor roles. Additionally, I included Sales Areas 0001/01/01 and 0002/01/01 in the customer role, serving as a template.

 

Implement BAdI /SHCM/EP_BP_ROLE_SYNC

Create a new BAdI Implementation for BAdI /SHCM/EP_BP_ROLE_SYNC. Various methods are provided, and I used the method /SHCM/IF_B_EP_BP_ROLE_SYNC~MODIFY_ALL because it encompasses the full set of data, including Sales Area information. Below is a coding example for your implementation, which illustrates the general functionality. Note that there may be additional side effects that need to be assessed in your implementation. This example serves as a valuable starting point.

  METHOD /shcm/if_b_ep_bp_role_sync~modify_all.
    CONSTANTS: lc_customer TYPE bu_partnerrole VALUE 'FLCU01'.

    DATA: ls_ptrv       TYPE ptrv_vendor_sync,
          lv_curr_bukrs TYPE bukrs,
          ls_cust_link  TYPE v_cvi_cust_link,
          lt_customers  TYPE cmds_ei_extern_t,
          ls_customers  TYPE cmds_ei_extern,
          ls_ref_bp     TYPE cmds_ei_main,
          ls_ref_cust   TYPE cmds_ei_main,
          lt_zrole      TYPE TABLE OF zbp_role_sync,
          ls_zrole      TYPE zbp_role_sync,
          lv_bukrs_new  TYPE bukrs,
          ls_sa_new     TYPE cmds_ei_sales_key,
          ls_cust_cc    TYPE cmds_ei_company,
          lv_knb1       TYPE knb1,
          ls_cust_sa    TYPE cmds_ei_sales,
          lv_knvv       TYPE knvv.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_customers> TYPE LINE OF cmds_ei_extern_t,
                   <fs_cust_cc>   TYPE cmds_ei_company,
                   <fs_cust_sa>   TYPE cmds_ei_sales.

* check if role FLCU01 is assigned
    READ TABLE cs_bp_data-partner-central_data-role-roles
      WITH KEY data_key = lc_customer
      TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS.

    IF sy-subrc = 0.
* customer sales role FLCU01 is relevant
* get reference BP
      lv_curr_bukrs = is_employee_data-org_assignment-bukrs.
      CLEAR ls_ptrv.
      SELECT SINGLE * FROM ptrv_vendor_sync INTO @LS_ptrv
        WHERE bukrs = @LV_curr_bukrs.

      IF ls_ptrv IS NOT INITIAL.
* get reference customer BP number
        SELECT SINGLE * FROM v_cvi_cust_link INTO @LS_cust_link
          WHERE partner = @LS_ptrv-ref_vendor.

* read reference customer data
        APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_customers ASSIGNING <fs_customers>.
        <fs_customers>-header-object_instance-kunnr = ls_cust_link-kunnr.
        <fs_customers>-header-object_task = 'U'.

        ls_ref_bp-customers = lt_customers.
        cmd_ei_api_extract=>get_data(
          EXPORTING
            is_master_data = ls_ref_bp
          IMPORTING
            es_master_data = ls_ref_cust ).

* data selection from z-table ZBP_ROLE_SYNC

        SELECT * FROM zbp_role_sync INTO TABLE @LT_zrole
          WHERE empcc = @LV_curr_bukrs.

        LOOP AT lt_zrole INTO ls_zrole.

          lv_bukrs_new = ls_zrole-addcc.
          ls_sa_new-vkorg = ls_zrole-vkorg.
          ls_sa_new-vtweg = ls_zrole-vtweg.
          ls_sa_new-spart = ls_zrole-spart.

          LOOP AT ls_ref_cust-customers INTO ls_customers.
* add additional company code
            LOOP AT ls_customers-company_data-company INTO ls_cust_cc.
              IF ls_cust_cc-data_key-bukrs = lv_bukrs_new.
                READ TABLE cs_bp_data-customer-company_data-company
                  WITH KEY data_key-bukrs = lv_bukrs_new
                  TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS.
                IF sy-subrc <> 0.
                  APPEND INITIAL LINE TO cs_bp_data-customer-company_data-company ASSIGNING <fs_cust_cc>.
                  MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_cust_cc TO <fs_cust_cc>.
                  <fs_cust_cc>-task = 'I'.
                  <fs_cust_cc>-data-pernr = is_employee_data-pernr.
                  <fs_cust_cc>-datax-pernr = abap_true.

                  SELECT SINGLE * FROM knb1 WHERE
                         kunnr = @CS_bp_data-customer-header-object_instance-kunnr AND
                         bukrs = @LV_bukrs_new
                  INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF @LV_knb1.

                  IF lv_knb1 IS NOT INITIAL.
                    <fs_cust_cc>-task = 'U'.
                  ENDIF.
                ENDIF.
              ENDIF.
            ENDLOOP.

* add additional sales area
            LOOP AT ls_customers-sales_data-sales INTO ls_cust_sa.
              IF ls_cust_sa-data_key = ls_sa_new.
                READ TABLE cs_bp_data-customer-sales_data-sales
                  WITH KEY data_key = ls_sa_new
                  TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS.
                IF sy-subrc <> 0.
                  APPEND INITIAL LINE TO cs_bp_data-customer-sales_data-sales ASSIGNING <fs_cust_sa>.
                  MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_cust_sa TO <fs_cust_sa>.
                  <fs_cust_sa>-task = 'I'.

                  SELECT SINGLE * FROM knvv WHERE
                         kunnr = @CS_bp_data-customer-header-object_instance-kunnr AND
                         vkorg = @LS_sa_new-vkorg AND
                         vtweg = @LS_sa_new-vtweg AND
                         spart = @LS_sa_new-spart
                    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF @LV_knvv.

                  IF lv_knvv IS NOT INITIAL.
                    <fs_cust_sa>-task = 'U'.
                  ENDIF.
                ENDIF.
              ENDIF.
            ENDLOOP.
          ENDLOOP.
        ENDLOOP.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

 

Role Synchronization

After completing these steps, you should be able to extend Employment Business Partners with additional Company Codes and Sales Areas as specified in your custom control table. Execute the report /SHCM/R_EMPL_HANDLE_BPROLES to perform role synchronization.

 

Verify Employment Business Partner

To verify the extended Employment Business Partner, use transaction /SHCM/EE_BP_RLTN.

For more detailed information about Employment Business Partner EE00000056, click on the Business Partner Number, leading you to transaction BP. Here, select role FLCU01 (Customer) and navigate to Sales and Distribution data.

As you can see, the system has added Sales Areas 0001/01/01 and 0002/01/01, incorporating all relevant data from the Reference Business Partner. You are now able to create Sales Orders for this Employee.

 

I hope this blog post was helpful for you. I'm looking forward to your feedback and thoughts or clarification questions you may have in the comment section below. 

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2505
entry.content:
Hello @Nils_Lutz ,
Yes, those were indeed incorrect. Thank you for pointing that out. I've removed them and will add the new ones.

Best regards

entry.title: Period of time in SD order item
entry.content:
Dear gurus,

 

is there any way to maintain a time period in which a sold service is provisioned?

 

I.e. we create an order item for a service fee from June 15th to July 10th. Is there a standard way to store these dates in the item and later print them on the invoice?

 

Cheers

Jens

entry.title: SAP CIS Administrators provisioning
entry.content:
Hi all

we add currently the Administrators in our SAP CIS tenant using this procedure: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/add-administrators#add-user-as-administrator

It is possible to create the CIS Administrators using Corporate IdP, like EntraID, as a source and automatically provision them to SAP CIS?

Best regards, Jacek

entry.title: Re: How to Prepare for the SAP BTP Solution Architect Certification (with Sample Questions)
entry.content:
Thanks so much, Dianna — really appreciate your kind words! 🙏
More to come soon — happy to support our community on the certification journey!
Amin

entry.title: Re: Automate dropping of local tables in SAP Datasphere w...
entry.content:
Tables created in DSP cannot be deleted using SQL commands. In fact, you cannot use DDL commands via  SQL on objects within the datasphere space.
You can do it in the OpenSQL schema if you enable it, but at that point, you will be using SAP HANA to manage them.

The only way to do it is through the DSP interface itself, or if you want to automate the process, the 'datasphere-cli' client allows you to operate on DSP objects and execute batch processes or automations using JavaScript, Python, or command line operations.

So, if you need to create/delete tables frequently, you might consider doing it from the OpenSQL schema (which must be enabled in the space administration, see Integrating Data via Database Users/Open SQL Schemas | SAP Help Portal), or alternatively, using the command-line client (@sap/datasphere-cli - npm)

Regards,

entry.title: Re: New member
entry.content:
Hi Chris!

Great to see your enthusiasm — and welcome to the community!
I’ve also found that there’s always something new to learn with Crystal Reports, no matter how long we’ve been using it 😄

If I may suggest — this could be a great opportunity to share a bit more about yourself. Don’t be shy to break the ice and open up! We’re all users, developers, and consultants working with the biggest ERP system in the world (SAP), and the community is all about connection and shared journeys.

If you'd like some inspiration, you can check out my own introduction post here:
👉 SAP, Curiosity & Me — Waldo’s Story
I’d love to hear what you think!

Here are my two cents (or my two grains of sand, as we’d say in Spanish 🇦🇷😉) — a few useful links to specific sections you might want to explore, depending on your area of interest:

  • SAP Crystal Reports
  • SAP Crystal Reports - SDK
  • SAP Crystal Reports viewer
  • SAP Crystal Reports, version for Eclipse
  • SAP Crystal Reports, version for Visual Studio
  • SAP Crystal Server

Each of these has Q&A, blog posts, and updates that can really help depending on how deep you want to go into the product or integration side.

Just a small tip: don’t forget to search before posting, as there’s a lot of valuable content already out there — and the community keeps evolving all the time. You might be surprised at what you’ll find!

Looking forward to learning alongside you!

Best,
Waldo

entry.title: Re: Integrate SAP CRM WEB UI in BTP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Hi Juliuspereira,

Thanks for your reply. Please find the below information:

1. Yes, we are accessing CRM system via Cloud Connector and given the root access for services.

2. Yes we are getting the login screen of CRM system after accessing the App and after authenticating the CRM system it is giving blank page with error attached earlier. Please note we have not enabled the Principal Propogation to see if it is hitting the CRM system.

 

3. PFA the Runtime destination setup screen shot along with App screen shot created in Build Work Zone site.

 

Please let me know if anything is missing.

 

Regards,

Rohit Gera

entry.title: Group currency value to be 0 in FX reval posting when document currency = group currency
entry.content:
Hi All,

I'm encountering an issue during foreign currency (FX) revaluation in SAP S/4HANA where the group currency (Group Curr) amount is not zero, even though the document currency group is the same as the group currency.

Scenario:
Company Code Currency: INR

Group Currency: USD

A journal entry is posted in USD (Document Currency & Group Currency = USD)

Issue:
When we run Foreign Currency Valuation, the system is generating FX revaluation entries with a non-zero amount in group currency. However, since the document currency group = group currency group, I expect group currency amount to be zero, as no currency translation should be necessary between same currencies.

Questions:
Is there a configuration or BAdI to modify this translation?

Is this a standard behavior or a known issue?

How can we suppress group currency FX revaluation when both document and group currencies are the same?

Any insights or recommendations would be appreciated.

entry.title: 📣‌ SAP Emarsys: Q2 2025 Product Release 📣‌
entry.content:
May has always been a significant month for SAP, and 2025 is no exception. As thousands of marketers from across the globe attend SAP Sapphire in Orlando, Madrid and virtually, the focus is once again on new innovations, AI solutions and real-time customer engagement. May also marks the arrival of the Q2 SAP Emarsys Product Release – a major milestone that consistently brings powerful new capabilities to the market. This quarter, we are unveiling innovative features designed to accelerate growth, enhance personalization and power personalized customer engagement across every channel.  

Let’s dive in.  

Deliver Omnichannel Excellence with Messaging, AI & Analytics Innovations  

Omnichannel continues to be a key element to creating connected customer experiences, which is reflected in many of the updates in this release. 

Mobile Wallet Enhancements 

Mobile Wallet Segmentation (GA): target specific groups of users with greater precision based on mobile wallet data, such as customers who haven't interacted with Mobile Wallet campaigns after a certain number of days.

Enhanced Campaign Distribution (GA): distribute wallet campaigns via Mobile Push and Mobile Inbox, giving you even more ways to reach customers.  

Pass Notifications (GA): Push an alert to all pass holders or specific customer segments about campaign updates using Automation Center.

Conversational Channel Updates  

WhatsApp: Template Messaging (GA): Create and automate one-way communications, such as promotional offers or order status updates, using the rich content, personalization and multi-language capabilities of the SAP Emarsys conversational channel.

Conversational Messaging for WhatsApp (EA): Engage in real-time conversations with your contacts based on their responses.  

Mobile App Engagement Updates:  

MAUI Binding for SAP Emarsys SDK (Pilot): Enable smooth integration of Mobile Engage into apps built with the .NET MAUI framework, providing IT greater flexibility and accelerating time-to-market.

Digital Ads Updates  

Microsoft Ads (Pilot): Leverage SAP Emarsys contact and behavioral data to build targeted ad audiences through segments or automations and extend your reach across Microsoft Search Network properties like Bing or Yahoo. 

Content Creation Updates 

Campaign List Page Unification (GA): Streamline navigation, offering a single interface to manage both batch and triggered campaigns with enhanced search, filtering and multi-selection for bulk actions. 

Predict Logic for Repeatable Blocks (Pilot): Use AI-powered Predict logics in repeatable VCE blocks for email to quickly scale personalized product recommendations across campaigns. Brand Center (EA): Brand Center for Omnichannel Content Blocks has entered the early adopter phase for Web Channel and Mobile In-app users with improved functionalities.  

AI-powered intelligence and personalization 

AI-assisted Product Finder (Pilot): Continuing in the next phase of piloting this release features enhanced natural language search and improved UI to simplify product curation and merchandising within campaigns for brands with an extensive catalog. 

AI-assisted Report Builder (Pilot): leverage the power of GenAI to generate custom reports addressing ad-hoc queries, empowering deeper data investigations and enhancing standard reporting. l

Analytics & Reporting 

Integrated Custom Reports (GA): View external dashboards and reports from third-party platforms, such as SAP Analytics Cloud and Google Looker Studio within the SAP Emarsys UI.

Enhanced Contact Search (Pilot): Enables partial matches for all fields, for example, searching "ric" in the first name field will return Richard, Patricia, and Cedric plus a new phone number search also covers the Phone, Phone (office), and Mobile fields. 

Data & Activity Summary (Pilot): Get an updated and expanded view of a contact profile that includes both Mobile Engage and Email engagement data.

Individual Link Tracking (GA): Access an updated report featuring a new name, simplified UI, improved export, and usage tracking to guide future enhancements. 

Grow & Adapt with our Extensible, Enterprise-Grade Platform Updates 

SAP Suite Integrations 

SAP Customer Data Cloud Connector: Topic Field (GA): Sync a new field type for 'Topics' from the SAP Customer Data Cloud Connector to Emarsys and use to enable more granular management of user subscriptions.

SAP Customer Data Platform Connector: Emarsys Mobile Activities Sync (EA): Expanded to include the sync of SAP Emarsys Mobile Push and In-app activities into SAP CDP for better insights and segmentation.

Standard Sales Data API  

Streaming Data (Pilot): Supports streaming data ingestion, allowing customers to send individual sales orders using JSON and make partial catalog updates versus wholesale ones.

Segmentation (Pilot): Build dynamic segments based on sales data using the intuitive, point-and-click segment editor.  

Engagement Events 

Event Configuration Status (Pilot): Adds new statuses for the Event Configurations to give customers more control and transparency during the setup process.  

Integration Monitor (Pilot): Confirm your event payloads are reaching the system and flag issues like failed imports, making troubleshooting faster and more efficient. 

Segmentation (Pilot):Target customers who submitted a negative product review for a specific product or brand within a set timeframe to reengage them. 

Event Attribution (Pilot): Measure specific goals and attributes which marketing channels and activities influenced those conversions.  

Product Catalog API 

Streaming Data (Pilot): Address the challenges of large and frequent catalog updates. It removes the 255-column limit and lets enterprise customers upload only the changed data, instead of re-uploading the entire catalog.

User Management via Identity Provider 

Flexible Role-Assignment (GA): Enable SAP Emarsys account owners to pre-configure user permissions based on Identity Provider (IdP) group fields and email addresses.

Improved First-Time Login (GA): Simplifies first-time SSO logins by automatically mapping user's role and access based on their email address, eliminating need for any manual setup.

Self-Serve Multi-Account Access Setup (GA): Enable connection of multiple SAP Emarsys accounts to a single Identity Provider using flexible SSO configurations.

UI Languages 

Traditional Chinese (GA): Toggle the SAP Emarsys UI to be in Traditional Chinese language, making the platform more accessible and inclusive. 

Thank you for exploring this product release with us. If you'd like to learn more about these features, how they work, and the value you can realize: 

  • Read the full product release blog 
  • Explore the full details on the product release page 
  • Register for the Q2 2025 Emarsys Release Webinar on June 5th  

And if you are interested in joining one of the active pilots or early adopter programs, please submit your request or reach out to your SAP Customer Success Partner (CSP) or SAP Emarsys Customer Success Manager (CSM).

entry.title: Re: Issue with MultiComboBox after upgrade to UI5 version 1.120.14
entry.content: It doesn't indicate the code that causes the problem so impossible to tell, so the best course of action would be to patch to 1.120.23 or higher as the note states.

entry.title: Re: Period of time in SD order item
entry.content:
Hi @jens_feldkamp1, 

You can use a sales contract instead of a sales order and maintain these dates in "contract start date" and "contract end date" on the item level. 

Best regards

entry.title: Invoice document - change/move of the positions
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I need to change the positions of some objects, for example sold-to party data, in the invoice document and create a customer specific solution. In the past it was possible to do this with the help of "Adobe Livecycle Designer". Unfortunately I found this tool just for SAP GUI use and I need a solution for SAP S/4HANA Fiori Public Cloud. 

Hopefully some of you know this problem and can help me to find a solution or give me some information where to download a version of the "Adobe Livecycle Designer" for SAP Fiori. It would be great if you have some other options to change/move fields in the invoice document.

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Different delivery types DIG DOG not possible in one document in MIGO
entry.content:
Hi

We have created Work Order in IW31 for Refurbishment Process. Then While Posting Goods Issue in MIGO with 261 Movement Type and Getting error.

Different delivery types DIG DOG not possible in one document.

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate road map viewer Now Accessible via SAP for Me
entry.content:
Followed the instruction for pop ups and cookies.  Worked when I made sure to add the me.sap.com to the cookies and check the box for third party cookies.  

Thanks for instruction.

entry.title: Re: Hey , Please can we delete a chart of account in SAP...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

Here's where you find more information: 

Adapting the Standard Chart of Accounts to Your Needs

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: How do you implement automatic surcharge capture?
entry.content:
You ever heard from this function?

ChatGPT:
To implement automatic surcharge capture in SAP Ariba Buying, you need to define Pricing Conditions or Rules within your catalog or through contracts. These rules specify how and when additional costs (e.g., shipping, customs) should apply. They can be configured in price sheets as part of catalog uploads or set via conditional logic tied to item attributes, regions, or suppliers—this requires activation of the Total Landed Cost (TLC) feature and possibly realm-level parameters.

User:
How do I set up the pricing rules?

ChatGPT:
Pricing rules are configured either through price sheets in catalog uploads (CSV/CIF templates) or via contract pricing conditions. In catalog uploads, you define rules using fields like additionalCostType, amount, currency, and application basis (e.g., per unit, percentage). For more advanced automation, use contract-based pricing tied to specific supplier agreements, which can trigger conditions based on volume, product type, or region. All of this requires the Total Landed Cost feature to be active in your realm.

User:
Where do I find this: Click "Add Price Sheet" or edit an existing one — or do I need a parameter activated for that?

ChatGPT:
The “Add Price Sheet” option is not always visible by default. To access or upload price sheets:

  1. Your user role must have Catalog Admin permissions.

  2. The Total Landed Cost feature must be enabled in your realm (via a parameter like Application.CustomCatalog.EnableLandedCost).

  3. Navigate to: Catalogs → Catalog Manager, select or create a catalog, then go to “Import” and click “Download Template” or “Add Price Sheet”.

If this functionality is not visible, you likely need SAP Support or your system admin to activate the appropriate parameters for your realm.

User:
How do I find the Price Sheet layout in Ariba?

ChatGPT:
To find the Price Sheet layout:

  1. Go to Catalogs → Catalog Manager.

  2. Choose an existing catalog or click “Create Catalog”.

  3. Under “Import”, click “Download Template” — this gives you the price sheet structure (CSV or Excel).

Alternatively, find documentation on SAP Help Portal – CIF Format.
If TLC fields like additionalCostType, additionalCost, etc., are missing, request the latest template from your SAP Ariba Support. These fields only appear if your realm has Total Landed Cost activated.

Example: Price Sheet (CSV) with TLC Surcharges

supplierPartID,description,unitPrice,currency,unitOfMeasure,additionalCost1Type,additionalCost1,additionalCost1Currency,additionalCost1Basis,region
1001,Standard Widget,50,EUR,EA,Shipping,5,EUR,Per Unit,DE
1002,Deluxe Widget,100,EUR,EA,Insurance,10,EUR,Percentage of Price,EU
1003,Heavy Machinery,1000,EUR,EA,Handling,100,EUR,Flat Rate,ALL

Field Explanation:

Column Description
supplierPartIDSupplier's internal item number
descriptionItem description
unitPriceBase item price
currencyCurrency (e.g., EUR)
unitOfMeasureUnit of measure (e.g., EA = Each)
additionalCost1TypeType of surcharge (e.g., Shipping, Insurance, Handling, etc.)
additionalCost1Value of the surcharge (e.g., 5 EUR or 10%)
additionalCost1CurrencyCurrency of the surcharge (usually same as currency)
additionalCost1BasisBasis for the surcharge: Per Unit, Percentage of Price, or Flat Rate
regionRegion where the surcharge applies (e.g., DE, EU, ALL)

We would kindly request your support for the following:

  1. Enable Total Landed Cost (TLC) functionality in our realm, if not already active – including the parameter (e.g., Application.CustomCatalog.EnableLandedCost or equivalent).

  2. Provide access to the Price Sheet layout template that supports TLC-related fields such as:

    • additionalCostType, additionalCost, currency, basis (e.g. per unit or % of price)

    • Support for regional application (region, country)

  3. Clarify where we can access the “Add Price Sheet” option in the Catalog Manager UI – and confirm whether this depends on specific configuration or user role permissions.

  4. Optionally, share the latest official documentation or specification for setting up TLC rules in catalog uploads (CIF or Excel), including sample templates.

 

You mentioned: "If you want, I can create a sample CSV for a typical TLC Price Sheet upload." — Yes.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Wow — amazing that this thread is still alive ! That just shows how strong and welcoming the SAP Community really is 🌍

I’m joining in from Argentina, and it’s always great to see the global diversity we have here.
If you’re curious, feel free to check out my own introduction post here:
👉 SAP, Curiosity & Me — Waldo’s Story
I’d love to hear your thoughts!

Also — if anyone reading this is from Latam or especially Argentina, don’t hesitate to send me a private message.
It would be great to connect and share experiences — I haven’t found a specific Latam sub-group in this English-speaking community yet, so we might as well start building the bridge ourselves 😄

Saludos from the southern cone 🇦🇷 
—Waldo

entry.title: Re: Managing Project Planning and Execution With Project Demand in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:

I would expect a purchase order where full qty is received would get status closed automatically.

entry.title: Re: DML Perform in Crystal Report
entry.content:
Please provide an example of what you are trying to do.

There are Crystal Reports User Function Libraries (UFLs) that allow Crystal formulas to trigger any SQL statement.

entry.title: Re: ERROR: Account Description in language EN is not main...
entry.content: You just have to type the bank name in that field in english thats all.

entry.title: Problem With FIGL02 query
entry.content:
Hello, I have a problem with report /IMO/V_FIGL02_Q00001. As you can see in picture, in red box is aggregated value of cells under it.

The problem is that cell marked with green, "-1.410,58" isn't included in SUM. Result in red box(thats parent node in hierarchy) should be -3.055.611,55 instead of -3.054.200,97.
This problem occurs only on production client. And antoher thing is that if I filter out only one row, even if it is not from same node, result gets back to normal -3.055.611,55 as it should be.

I checked the data, checked the key figures, filters, nothing makes sense. 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Rebilling of ordered items via customer project
entry.content:
Hi Guys

I need your help! Can someone explain how it is possible to recharge services or materials to a customer based on a supplier invoice that was assigned to a customer project?

I have assigned the purchase order—and thus the supplier invoice—to the relevant WBS element, and I can also see the costs reflected in the project reporting. However, I am unable to recharge these costs to the customer.

Thank you in advance!

Maik

entry.title: Re: How to make the Component view Grayed-out in T-code C...
entry.content:
Hello @27maheboob 

A straightforward option is @RAHEEL_BABAR proposal to use screen variants. That will work, however is not plant-specific.

Another option is to use a BAdI enhancement at the time of production order save. Check if components are changed and raise an exception to prevent such changes. That can be made plant specific. The downside is that it won't to disable the component view, but it will prevent component changes, which I think is the ultimate goal.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How can I add a new SAP Managed Tag for blog posts and edit existing tags?
entry.content: how to know what are the available SAP managed tag?

entry.title: Re: Integrate SAP CRM WEB UI in BTP Build Work Zone
entry.content: And you have chosen paths and all sub paths in the CC correct? Have you also done clickjacking config in your CRM system? I believe that has something to do with the iframe error. Do you see any errors in the network tab? Anyone that is red in color?

So my question is, will the entries already existing in the production system be deleted if this change takes effect?

Thank you

entry.title: XNAB enhancement
entry.content:
How can we do enhancement in XNAB to split the records as per custom logic as per below. This is in the international version (99). We only need to do enhancement no configuration possible due to client setup. We have only one Valuation rule and need to split as below using BADI or any enhancement.

        Full Pay for the first 15 days – Valuation rule 01 

        Half pay for the next 20 days – Valuation rule 05 

        No pay after 35 days – Valuation rule 04

entry.title: Re: #CoE#GDTD Failed to create CDS of type 399
entry.content:
Hi angela

我已经删除了SL_CD1去尝试发布cds，

 

但是发布之后，cds却没有变化

所以我想是否是我的操作有问题，我的操作已经放在前面，您可以帮忙检查一下。

entry.title: SAP QM- Sample adjustment of Quality inspection lot if the previous lot was rejected
entry.content:
The business requirement is  get the sample size for my inspection lot increase or decrease based on the UD of the previous inspection lot for the same material batch.

For that I am using sampling procedure (which is linked to a sampling schema) and associated it with a dynamic modification rule that I created for this purpose.

Master data

My DMR is at “For usage decision” and  has two inspection stages with inspection severity- stage 10 for  4 (normal) and stage 20 for 6(Tightened). 10 is the initial stage and the condition is that for every 2 inspections done, if one gets rejected, then the stage should change to 20 which means tightened inspection.

I am assigning the DMR in the QM view of the material master for the inspection type.

Sampling schema

In my sampling schema , there are 3 tables :

  1. Inspec. Severity 04 + AQL 100
  2. Inspec. Severity 04 + AQL 1
  3. Inspec. Severity 06 + AQL 1

Each of these tables has the sample size etc. maintained for it.

Sampling procedure

However, in my sampling procedure  when I assign the sampling schema, there is a tab called “additional data” where system forces me to choose a sampling table from the sampling schema.  This is mandatory.

Problem:-

  • What I want is that based on the no. of acceptance or rejection of the previous inspection lots of the same material batch, it should choose the specific sampling table to decide the sample size for the new inspection lot created.
  • I am noticing that irrespective of whether the previous Inspection lot(s) for the material is accepted or rejected, it always decides the sample size as per the sampling table specified in the additional data only, of the sampling procedure.
  • Also, I am not sure as how to factor in the batch thing. For different batches of the same material, the sample size need not increase. But if the same batch had a lot which got rejected the last time, the sample size for the new inspection lot should increase. In this case, I am wanting that stage 20 of DMR should kick in and the sample size I have specified for the stage 20

E,g:- If the previous lot for same batch got rejected, then when new GR happens and second Ilot is created, then it should have a sample size as per inspection severity 6 (Tightened).

Regards,

Anji

Best Regards,

Priyanka

Kind regards,

Andrzej

entry.title: SAP Experts: Are We Still Building for the Past, or Architecting for the Clean Core Future?
entry.content:
Beyond the Buzzwords: Making SAP S/4HANA Customization Less Scary

Ever wished your SAP system could do just a little bit more? Maybe you need a specific field on a customer record that isn't there by default, or you want a report to show data in a slightly different way. This is where the magic of extensibility comes in – and in SAP S/4HANA, the "Custom Fields and Logic" app is your new best friend.

Think of it this way: SAP provides you with an incredibly powerful, pre-built house (your S/4HANA system). Extensibility is like having the tools and blueprints to add new rooms, change the paint color, or even build a custom-designed garage – all without tearing down the existing structure. It's about adapting SAP to your unique business needs, rather than adapting your business to SAP.

Your Extensibility Command Center: The "Custom Fields and Logic" App

At the heart of this flexibility lies the Custom Fields and Logic app in SAP Fiori. This intuitive, web-based tool empowers key users (people who understand the business processes but might not be traditional developers) to make significant changes without needing to write complex code from scratch.

This single app brings together three core extensibility superpowers:

  1. Custom Fields: Your "New Sticky Notes" for SAP
  2. Data Source Extensions: Unlocking "Hidden" Information
  3. Custom Logic: Your "Smart Rules" for Business Processes

Let's break them down for dummies.

1. Custom Fields: Adding Your Own Data Points

Remember our "sticky note" analogy? Custom fields are exactly that! If you need to store specific information that SAP's standard fields don't cover (like "Customer Segment," "Project Lead," or a "Special Approval Status"), you can create your own.

How it works (Simplified):

  • Go to the "Custom Fields and Logic" app.
  • Choose "Custom Fields" tab.
  • Create a new field: Give it a name, decide what kind of data it holds (text, number, checkbox), and choose where you want it to appear (e.g., on a customer's details screen, in a report).
  • Enable Usage: Tell SAP where this field should be available – in Fiori apps, reports, email templates, or even for search.
  • Publish: Make your changes live. It's that simple!

You can even define how these fields should behave when data is aggregated (like summing up numbers or finding the maximum value).

2. Data Source Extensions: Revealing Existing Information

Sometimes, the data you need already exists in SAP, but it's not being pulled into the specific report or app you're using. Data Source Extensions allow you to "expose" these existing standard fields.

How it works (Simplified):

  • Go to the "Custom Fields and Logic" app.
  • Switch to the "Data Source Extensions" tab.
  • Select the data source (the "pool" of information) you want to extend.
  • Enable additional standard fields: Pick from a list of fields that are already technically available but just not "active" in that specific data source.
  • Publish: Your report or app will now show this previously "hidden" data.

This is super useful for enriching existing reports or views with information that's already in your system.

3. Custom Logic: Teaching SAP New Tricks (with Guard Rails!)

This is where things get really powerful – and surprisingly user-friendly. Custom Logic allows you to implement your own business rules, validations, or calculations directly within SAP. Think of it as telling SAP: "If X happens, then do Y."

How it works (Simplified):

  • Go to the "Custom Fields and Logic" app.
  • Switch to the "Custom Logic" tab.
  • Choose a business context: This defines when your custom logic will run (e.g., when a sales order is saved, or a customer record is updated).
  • Write your logic: You use a simplified, web-based editor with "ABAP for Key Users." This is a restricted version of SAP's programming language (ABAP) that makes it easy to write simple rules without worrying about complex coding pitfalls. It's like building sentences with a predefined set of words.
  • Publish: Your new rule is now active!

Important Note on "Restricted ABAP": Don't be scared by "ABAP"! This "restricted" version is specifically designed for safety and ease of use. It allows you to read data, perform calculations, and implement basic conditions, but prevents you from doing anything that could destabilize the system, like directly messing with the database or creating new programs. It's all about empowering you safely.

Beyond the App: Custom Database Fields

What if you have custom fields that were already created in the SAP backend (perhaps by developers before S/4HANA Fiori apps became central)? The system allows you to "enable" these existing backend database fields for use within the Fiori "Custom Fields and Logic" app (using a transaction like SCFD_EUI). Once enabled, they can be managed and utilized just like the custom fields you create directly in the Fiori app.

Transporting Your Innovations

Once you've created your custom fields or logic, you'll want them to be available in your live production system. SAP provides apps like "Configure Software Packages" and "Register Extensions for Transport" to safely move your innovations from development to testing and finally to production. You can also view all your extensions and their relationships in the "Extensibility Inventory."

The Bottom Line

The "Custom Fields and Logic" app in SAP S/4HANA is a game-changer for businesses looking to truly leverage their ERP system without heavy reliance on traditional development cycles. It empowers key users to adapt and extend standard functionalities, making SAP more responsive to evolving business needs.

So, next time someone talks about "extensibility," remember: it's just about having the right tools to make your SAP system perfectly fit your business, one custom field or smart rule at a time!

What aspect of SAP S/4HANA would you like to see demystified next? Let me know in the comments!

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_sap-s4hana-business-partner-field-enhancementdocx-activity-7331314548240445442-gcQR?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAAJPDL8BjpJe3thZB6t04k7Z2hRVHhboAec

entry.title: Re: Request for Guidance on SAP PS (Public Cloud) for Con...
entry.content: Look into the SAP Store for Partner Solutions.  SAP recommends adding Smartapp to S/4HANA Public Cloud for EC&O customers.  The Enterprise Project structure usually set up as a CBS.

Some KBA's that I have read suggest that this can't be done. Is there a way to get this information from SF?

Thanks

Does anyone know what i'm doing wrong? Thanks in advance

entry.title: i don't need the line no in my Web intelligence SAP BO report
entry.content: I am getting the Line number and didn't get the consolidated values, how do i remove the Line number

entry.title: Cost Optimization for SAP Cloud-Based Environments
entry.content:
The transition from on-premises to cloud infrastructure brings numerous advantages, including scalability, flexbility, and enhanced performance. However, these benefits can be undermined by poor resource management, lack of cost visibility, and inefficient configurations. To address these challenges, organizations must adopt targeted strategies that not only reduce costs but also ensure optimal system performance and reliability. This article delves into key techniques for cost optimization in SAP Cloud Infrastructure, including resource right-sizing, auto-scaling policies, and the use of Reserved Instances and Savings Plans. It also highlights the importance of leveraging advanced monitoring tools to track resource usage and identify cost-saving opportunities.

This article explains how to Reduce the cost of SAP cloud-based servers (like SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, or running SAP on IaaS, QA or Dev providers like AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud) depends on how your environment is set up and what your needs are.

  • Shut Down Non-Production Systems When Not in Use
  • Clean Up Unused Systems or Services
  • Monitor Usage with Cloud Cost Tools

 Shut Down Non-Production Systems When Not in Use:-

 Use automation or scheduling tools to stop Dev, QA, and sandbox systems during off-hours (nights/weekends).

A Stop/Start policy automatically stops AWS EC2 instances at a scheduled time and starts them again later. This helps save costs since stopped instances don’t incur compute charges, though you still pay for EBS volumes.

 Example: EventBridge + Lambda Setup for AWS

 

  1. Create a Lambda Function

Python example to stop EC2 instances:

  1. Create an EventBridge Rule
    • Use cron expressions like:
      • Stop at 7 PM UTC: cron(0 19 * * ? *)
      • Start at 7 AM UTC: cron(0 7 * * ? *)

 

This function initiates the EC2 instance at 9:00 AM IST. We can configure the same scheduling pattern to stop the instance according to our specified times.

For: Azure VM

To schedule start and stop of Azure VMs, you can utilize Azure Automation’s “Start/Stop VMs during off-hours” feature or Logic Apps. Azure Automation allows you to create an Automation Account, deploy a solution, and configure schedules and runbooks to automate the start and stop processes. Logic Apps offer a visual designer to build workflows that integrate with Azure VM resources.

 

Imp Note : If the QA, Dev, and S4HANA schedules have started but the SAP MMC is not functioning to resolve this issue, please refer to the article :

 this article explains how to automatically start the SAP Management Consol (SAP Service) when booting up the SAP cloud or physical system. Generally, to ensure that any program starts automatically during the boot process, we need to add that application to the scheduled tasks and assign a specific time for it to launch. However, while the SAP Management application may open, the SAP necessary services will not start automatically and will need to be initiated manually.

 After installing the SAP application, we need to check two settings in the Windows Services.

 Step 1:

 If the startup type is (Automatic Delayed Start) in Windows Services, the option will only start the application but will not initiate the SAP service. To run the SAP service, we must log into the system and start the service manually using the service username and password.

Step 2

 If the service associated with the account name and instance name does not start at startup, we need to log into the system and manually start the SAP MMC service.

 Solution for this issue:  We should select two options: one is Type: Automatic on General Tab, and the other is to log on with the.\SAP service.

For S4 HANA:

After starting the instance or virtual machine (VM) in AWS, Azure, or any cloud server, the HANA database and application do not start automatically. We must start them manually using commands by connecting to the system with PuTTY, where we execute the database and application start commands. The information below pertains to how to configure automatic startup when the system is initiated in the cloud at a specific time.

Connect the system thorough PuTTY.

# cd /opt

# sudo vi start-hana.sh   ( Copy the below code and paste it on start-hana.sh file)

 

# sudo vi stop-hana.sh   ( Copy the below code and paste it on stop-hana.sh file)

 After saving the file. Go to shell and execute the below commands.

 

Clean Up Unused Systems or Services

 Decommission old systems, backups, or logs that are no longer needed.

  • Archive historical data to lower-cost storage tiers (e.g., Amazon S3 Glacier, Azure Cool Blob Storage).

Monitoring Usage and Cost

 Cost Allocation & Tagging

  •          Use tags or labels to allocate costs by project, department, or team.
  • Budgets & Alerts
  •          Define spending limits and trigger notifications when thresholds are breached.
  • Forecasting & Trend Analysis
  •          Predict future usage and budget accordingly.
  • Usage Anomaly Detection
  •          Identify spikes or unexpected changes in usage.
  • Rightsizing & Recommendations
  •          Suggest instance downsizing or idle resource termination.

entry.title: Re: Demystifying Document Management Service on BTP
entry.content:
Hi Sundeep,

Thanks for this great blog – we followed your guide and were able to successfully create ArchiveLink attachments and save the documents to the CMIS repository.

However, when using the ARCHIVELINK_MIGRATION report to migrate existing ArchiveLink attachments to the CMIS repository, the documents are migrated, but without content (file size is zero).

Could you please advise how we can resolve this?

Best regards,
Abdulfattah Hussein

You mentioned that Part 3 would cover configuration details for Key Users, Functional Consultants, and UX Architects. I’d love to read that — is it published yet, or could you please share the link if available? Looking forward to diving into the next part!

entry.title: Workers with same email ID in manage workforce app
entry.content:
How to create workers with same email ID in manage workforce app?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Workers with same email ID in manage workforce app
entry.content:
There is a customizing option which can be found in configuration activity 103515 - Business User Field Validation is available where you can make the validation for the Email address.

3549440 - How to disable unique email id check - Manage Workforce

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
@Ankush_Modi @Sahil7Sharma @miriam_braz  hi community members! I'm thrilled to meet you. I live in the US outside of Philadelphia. Here are some hints on getting engaged. I wish you the best and an amazing SAP career.

Join groups and get engaged / network!

  • Many interest groups https://community.sap.com/t5/interest-groups/ct-p/interests
  • Many location groups (or become an event organizer yourself!) https://community.sap.com/t5/location-groups/ct-p/location
  • Met the SAP Champions and SAP Mentors as well as follow them on social media https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-community-leaders-finder/bg-p/influencers-directory

entry.title: Re: Performance Rating and Comments Upload via Integration Centre in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi! 

Thanks for the clear documentation! 🙂 

I just have a question: our client wants that managers can update individual ratings within an objective section on the performance form for their different direct reports (at once - because now managers should navigate to each form seperately).

However, with this solution, I don't think it's feasible to use this intergration center job as you will perform it on behalf of the managers, right? This means that you need to perform this job for every manager that wants to update the ratings? 

Thx!

entry.title: Re: Period of time in SD order item
entry.content: Billing Plan is one of the best options

entry.title: Re: Bi weekly/Weekly Notifications for pending Time Sheet in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi @harinich 

I would suggest troubleshooting this rule in below ways 

1) Create a rule trace for the above rule

2) Create the manage data custom Object manually for that user and save.  

With this you would know the reason why your rule is not working. Perhaps the lookup condition needs to be checked if you are specifying it as -5 days.

entry.title: Layout characters limitation Business Objects 4.3
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working with a Freehand SQL report in SAP BusinessObjects. After upgrading from version 4.2 to 4.3, I noticed a limitation when using a BigQuery (BQ) connection: string columns seem to be truncated or limited to 128 characters.

This issue does not occur when using an Oracle connection with VARCHAR(1000) on version 4.2 — everything works as expected there.

As a temporary workaround, I managed to split the string into 8 substrings of 128 characters each and then concatenated them in a separate variable. This allows me to retrieve the full content, but it's obviously not an ideal or scalable solution.

Has anyone experienced similar behavior with BQ connections in BO 4.3? Is this a known limitation, a configuration issue, or potentially a bug introduced in the newer version?

Any guidance or workaround suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Transport Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 3-System Landscape: From Basics to Comp
entry.content: @ylo  In most situation delta changes are deployed. Only for some specific system situations a synchronisation of all objects is performed. Please open a ticket if you have always long import times so that the situation can be checked in detail.

entry.title: Re: Overcoming Challenges in Staffing Customer Projects with SAP Project and Resource Management
entry.content:
Hello Shahana,

thanks for raising your questions. Below some feedback.

1. Overstaffing / understaffing is not restricted and allowed and should be tracked at request level and/or maybe at the project level in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. A resource manager can also set a request to the status "Resolved" afterwards staffing cannot be changed, but please assess this option carefully as possibly an update triggered from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition could also be blocked. 

2. The current logic how assigned hours are distributed is documented in the following document:
https://help.sap.com/docs/PPRM_OD/afef0d26c811418aaebe8878e0a3cbaa/f57b86cf019b4869b7705074e91add53.html 

Thank you, Miguel

entry.title: Re: Restrict option to cascade goals to certain group of employees
entry.content: We would also be interested in such an option. I assume the cascading is not respecting the Target population set via RBP. Could this be changed?

entry.title: Re: Is 'Price per User per Month' Model Possible in SAP B...
entry.content:
Hello

ByD Does have the functionality For Price per period, Can you check if that works for you.

more information on this is available in : Invoice Schedule | SAP Help Portal

Also check : 
2527596 - Cannot activate Price per Period Indicator for a Product in a Contract



Regards,
Harshal

entry.title: Filter items in a section based on a selected or checked item in another section on an Object Page
entry.content:
Greetings, community experts.

I'm working on a SAP CAP + SAP Fiori Elements application, which has an Object Page with several sections (all Tables).

I need to filter the table elements in the lower sections based on which item (row) is selected or highlighted in the table in the first section. The following image illustrates the idea a little more.

The goal is for a user to be able to click on a row in a table (in the example image, I clicked on the first row of the first table... that's why the line appears highlighted with a dotted box), and based on that, I can apply certain filters to the subsequent tables.

I don't know if what I'm suggesting is possible in SAP Fiori Elements, or where to start.

Any help, suggestions, related materials, or even proposed alternative approaches would be appreciated in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP CHOTA Talks Q2 2025: A Resounding Success and a Glimpse into the Future of SAP in Hyderabad!
entry.content: @MaheshCV Thank you so much! Really appreciate your session, it was insightful and inspiring, Sir!

entry.title: Re: Can't we use the same source objects if it is already...
entry.content: Why do you need it? Basically you double the data. This is contrary to the single read principle. Anyway you can bypass by creating second connection (from second space) and then share the table.

entry.title: Re: Offer letter text and tokens are appearing as red color
entry.content:
Hi @sudheer_y ,

As of now, there’s no major functional change announced by SAP SuccessFactors regarding this color behavior. It has generally been a part of the token parsing logic in the Offer Letter UI. However, if this started recently after an upgrade or release, you may want to check:

SAP SuccessFactors Release Notes

Admin Center > Upgrade Center > Recently Applied Changes

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: End-to-End SAP Integration: Plant Maintenance Meets Warehouse Management
entry.content:
@GowthamParasa great summary of the process. one note is that when it comes to the delivery number in EWM that is generated, you do have the ability to use the outbound delivery number in S/4HANA as the delivery number in EWM. this helps with reducing the amount of different document numbers that a user might need to reference throughout the process.

you can check this box below to use the outbound delivery from S/4HANA in EWM:

entry.title: Re: Build, Deploy, and Extend AI Agents with Joule Studio
entry.content: Does Joule relies on Model Context Protocol (MCP) ?

entry.title: Re: Is 'Price per User per Month' Model Possible in SAP B...
entry.content: Thanks, but as stated above :

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch - ABAPConf 2025 Edition
entry.content: So gut, dass wir uns schon am Vorabend treffen! Wie üblich musst Du bitte beim Stammtisch Dein Essen und die Getränke selbst bezahlen. Während der Konferenz übernimmt dass dann ADventas.

entry.title: Re: Is 'Price per User per Month' Model Possible in SAP B...
entry.content: Thanks, but as stated above : Quantity as a time unit (e.g. Month): If we define the quantity as a number of months and check the “Price by Period” option, the system correctly understands that the price should be billed monthly. However, in this case, we lose the link to the actual number of users, so there’s no way to multiply a “price per user per month” by the number of users.

entry.title: Re: Automate dropping of local tables in SAP Datasphere w...
entry.content: Appreciate your quick response and help. Will try to explore both solutions.

entry.title: Re: Failed to get application list from Mobile Services w...
entry.content: It may be that BAS is connected to a different Org/Space that doesn't contain Mobile Services.  I would run the "CF: Set Org and Space" command and verify you are logged into the correct endpoint and have selected the right Org and Space.

entry.title: How to add extra fields as editable fields to a RAP BO?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm looking for a way to add additional fields to the behavior definition of a RAP BO so that we can change those fields from inside that object. To simply display data we have virtual elements in the projection layer, but in this case we want the fields to be editable. The changes could be saved to an external table via an additional save or unmanaged save, but the tricky thing here seems to be how to add these extra fields to the behavior definition?

Why this question: for example, why I'm looking into this, in HR some long texts are stored as HRPxxxx TLINE tables. Since we don't want to have the user edit these texts, truncated line per line in a table, we need to have a string field in our BO which contains the concatenated version of the HR table data.  In addition it might also be necessary to introduce an etag field for existing tables without admin data, if we want to make that BO draft enabled.

Some approaches I've been looking into so far:

- Use a custom entity as behavior definition entity: unfortunately no draft nor projection layer available with this approach and unmanaged scenario

- Do an outer join with an (empty) z-table which contains the additional fields in the view that is used by the behavior definition: this can actually work but it feels a bit creative. By implementing an edit with additional implementation (here we read the HR table, convert from TLINE tab to string, and set that value in our draft) + an additional/unmanaged save to write back the updated string as TLINE tab in the HR table.

So since my field does not literally exist in any db table, even via the full unmanaged scenario I didn't find a way to solve it in an elegant way. What I'm looking for is a recommended approach to have virtual (changeable) fields in the definition layer instead of the available read-only ones in the projection layer.

Any advice? 🙂

Thanks in advance!

KR,

Bjorn

entry.title: Re: National ID integration in between Recruitment Management and Onboarding 2.0
entry.content:
Hi @RajendraPrasadAllam 

This is very useful post, thank you!

Do you know which EC rules can be impacted by the script? We have some onSave and onInit rules under National id portlet, but they do not make changes to other entities based on national id condition, they validate/pre-populate nat id fields.

Evgeniia

entry.title: Re: Enabling Dynamic Fiscal filtering in SAC (BW Live)
entry.content: This does not allow the user to chose a dynamic time reference of their own. They are entirely dependent on BW having the setup for manual time references, this doesn't feel dynamic. Is there not a way to simply have a date field where the SAC system can recognize it as such and allow the SAC developer to select a dynamic time reference similar to the acquired model accessbility?

entry.title: Re: Create Sales Orders - Automatic Extraction (II): One-Click Setup for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello 

We are on S/4HANA 2023 FSP02 - On premise demo environment.

We get above same error. How to resolve the same for on-premise environment?

Thanks

Santosh

entry.title: Re: How to add extra fields as editable fields to a RAP BO?
entry.content: you should try unmanaged bo

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors WorkZone Page Load Times

entry.title: Hi,I have a question. If for a Output type Medium is not Print output then output won't print?
Re: SAP SuccessFactors WorkZone Page Load Times
entry.content: Thank you, John for getting back to me! We are keeping the fingers crossed... If we get any news from SAP, I will update you here

entry.title: Re: 未本地化國家的公司幣別如何設定？
entry.content:
Here's the machine translation:

How is the company currency set for an unlocalized country?

Hi Expert,
Only Germany can be selected because the company configuration company was not localized, but the company currency cannot be changed automatically to EUR.
Two questions:
1. How do I change my company to Cayman?
2. How do I change my company to USD after I set up my company?

entry.title: Is Data extraction from SAP SF-ICM to SAP datasphere in delta possible
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Is delta extraction from SAP SuccessFactors-ICM (Incentive Compensation Management) to SAP Datasphere possible, wanted to pull the tables that starts with "CS_'.

When I tried adding the CS_* tables in the Replication flow of SAP Datasphere, with delta mode as "Initial and Delta" it is getting failed. 

"Initial only" is working fine for these tables, wanted to check whether delta support is available/any approach to pull the data in delta mode.

Can someone help on this. Thanks!

--

KRPK

SAP Datasphere SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management

entry.title: Build Apps Mashup in FSM
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently trying to figure out how to show a mashup of an Build Apps application in FSM Web Container.
As far as I am concerned - because Web Containers send an POST request with a payload - Build Apps application blocks this call, because it can only handle GET request. 
As a workaround I can redirect the POST request as an GET request via CPI to the application, but I was wondering if there is an easier way to do it.

Kind regards,
Kevin

entry.title: Cannot reach s4dhost.wdf.sap.corp . "error code: 503; Message : Service Unavailable"
entry.content:
Hi

I am taking course "SAP Integration Suite". In chapter "Creating an API Provider" at https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-with-sap-integration-suite/creating-an-api-provider_b95113e7-369f-4dd3-9773-ec4e0fde2e00 I create an API Provider.

My configuration in Connection is:
Host: s4dhost.wdf.sap.corp
Port: 8080
Location ID : st-u45s-cld900dev
Authentication : none
sap-client : 100

My configuration in Catalog Service Settings is:
Path prefix: /sap/opu/odata
Service Collection URL : /IWFND/CATALOGSERVISE;=2/ServiceCollection
Catalog URL : http://s4dhost.wdf.sap.corp:8080/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVISE;=2/ServiceCollection
Authentication type : BASIC
Username: <my email-user>  <- I also tried with my SAP ID : "P1......" . Same error
Password : <...>


When I test connection I get error : "Connection to the system was unsuccessful with HTTP response code: 503; Message : Service Unavailable"


Can You help me point out where the error reside in my configuration?

 

/Regards

Joakim

entry.title: Re: Failed to get application list from Mobile Services w...
entry.content: Thanks Bill. Indeed had to set the orgs and space again. Wondering how that got lost, but is working now again.

entry.title: Re: 未本地化國家的公司幣別如何設定？
entry.content: Read KBA 3467757 - Change the country, currency and chart of accounts for a company

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
@Alex_Martin did you get any solution for the Rehire scenario?

Regards

entry.title: Re: Can't we use the same source objects if it is already...
entry.content:
We need to load ACDOCA with the relevant CDS View into our Datasphere. The initial load is too big when we try to do it for the last four years, and it always fails.

Initially, we loaded each historic year as a full load and do a delta only for the current year. However, the full loads / initials take around eight hours.

We then realised that the customer has changes in ACDOCA for previous years too, which is why it isn't enough to only have the current year as a delta.

However, regularly loading every year as a full load with an 8-hour runtime is also not a viable solution.

That's why we plan to use several replication flows, each for one year, and use the delta for historic years as well.

entry.title: Re: 未本地化國家的公司幣別如何設定？
entry.content: Read this answer: Deployment of S4HC to a country where there is no country version provided by SAP?

entry.title: Unable to delete Application Job Catalog on ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I ran an ABAP class for creating an application job catalog.

Unfortunetly, the transport request number was wrong, so the creation failed.

I tried to put the right transport request number, but the creation of the application job catalog fail with the error :

Creation of job catalog entry failed: Object ZMY_OBJECT (type job catalog entry) already exists.

However, there is no job catalog under the Application Job Catalog Entries folder, neither in the IAM Apps folder.

Is there a way to do a clean delete of the catalog ? Or do I have to create a new catalog with a new name ?

Thanks in advance,

Rémy

entry.title: External email and attachment to new hire referees in onboarding 2.0
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am curious if there are any work-arounds to trigger an email and attachment to external users email address  such as the former employer or new hire referees from the onboarding module. The static/dynamic document in email services work for just the onboardee and responsible groups - while I have lean hope that this is possible, I thought to check if anyone has achieved something close to this requirement. Otherwise, this is classified as absolutely outside-of-the-system task. Suggestions will be appreciated, thank you.

Rac.

entry.title: Re: Is 'Price per User per Month' Model Possible in SAP ByDesign Contracts?
entry.content:
So you want something such as

  • a Sales Contract that includes:
    • A recurring product or service (e.g., "User License")
    • Quantity-based pricing (e.g., number of users)
    • Monthly billing cycle

But SAP Business ByDesign seems to require some time unit and only fixed price, so not sure this is possible 😟

entry.title: C_TS4FI_2021 - SAP Certified Application Associate - SAP S/4HANA for Financial Accounting Associates
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently part of the SAP Learning Group as a student and using the Student Edition. I am very interested in the SAP S/4HANA Finance – Financial Accounting certification and would like to know if it is possible to take this certification as a student with access to the Student Edition.

Could you please let me know if this is feasible and, if so, what the requirements or procedures are?

Additionally, I noticed that most of the course content is in English, but I am not fluent in English. Is there a way to follow the course in French or German, and is it also possible to take the certification exam in one of these languages?

Thank you in advance for your time and support.

Kind regards,
Fabrice

entry.title: Re: Where can you find open APIs for SAP Cloud ALM?
entry.content:
Hi, Indra0,

You can find help documentation for Cloud ALM's API at: API Guide for SAP Cloud ALM | SAP Help Portal

There is a site, SAP Business Accelerator Hub, where you can use the various APIs in a sandbox -> documentation: Trying Out APIs in a Sandbox Environment | SAP Help Portal or connect to your Cloud ALM -> documentation: Trying Out APIs in a Configured Environment | SAP Help Portal

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: Inbound delivery released after packing not appearing...
entry.content: Hi@Ajit_Routray

entry.title: Internal Logistics - Consumption to Cost Center - SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there any possibility of including the GL account as an optional field in the following SAP Business ByDesign transactions?

Internal Logistics > Consumption for Cost Center

Physical Inventory > New Inventory Adjustment

Looking forward to your insights.

Thank you!
